about.hero_subtitle: On a mission to reduce food waste in Uzbekistan
about.mission_text: Foody Moody was created to reduce food waste in Uzbekistan and connect consumers with quality products at affordable prices. We aim to bring savings to every household, extra revenue to businesses, and less waste to the environment.
about.story_text: Foody Moody was founded in 2024 by Makhliyo Shodieva and Sarvar Abdullaev. While studying the global food waste crisis, we discovered that millions of tonnes of perfectly good food are thrown away every day — simply because it's near its expiry date. We saw that technology had already started solving this problem in Europe, and realized Central Asia had no such solution. So we built Foody Moody — a platform that connects local businesses with customers, turning potential waste into affordable meals. Our goal is to make saving food as easy as ordering it.
about.vision_text: In the future, Foody Moody plans to expand across all of Uzbekistan and then into other Central Asian countries, significantly reducing food waste across the region.
about.sec2_title: Millions of products are wasted every day
about.sec2_body: In Uzbekistan, bakeries, restaurants and supermarkets throw away products with expiring dates every single day. This is not just an economic loss — it is a serious environmental issue.
about.sec4_body: Through Foody Moody, thousands of families have saved money, hundreds of businesses have earned extra income, and countless products have been rescued from waste.

contact.hero_subtitle: Have a question or suggestion? We'd love to hear from you.
contact.form_success_text: Thank you! We'll get back to you within 24 hours.

food_waste.hero_subtitle: The truth about food waste in Uzbekistan and the world — and how technology can fix it.
food_waste.global_title: The world wastes food at a staggering scale
food_waste.g_stat1_label: Tonnes of food wasted globally every year
food_waste.g_stat2_label: Of all food produced is thrown away
food_waste.g_stat3_label: Of global greenhouse gas emissions come from food waste
food_waste.g_stat5_label: People go hungry while food is wasted
food_waste.g_stat6_label: More water is wasted producing uneaten food than all human drinking water
food_waste.uz_subtitle: Based on research published on ScienceDirect and reports by FAO and Uzbekistan Ministry of Ecology.
food_waste.uz_rest_headline: An average restaurant in Uzbekistan wastes 12 tonnes of food per year
food_waste.uz_rest_source: Source: ScienceDirect research on food waste in Uzbek restaurants
food_waste.uz_fact1_sub: Each restaurant discards an average of 33 kg of food every single day
food_waste.uz_fact2_sub: Small establishments waste 3 to 7 tonnes of food annually
food_waste.uz_fact3_sub: Medium-sized restaurants waste 10 to 23 tonnes per year
food_waste.uz_fact4_sub: Large establishments can waste up to 30 tonnes of food annually
food_waste.uz_fact5_sub: Overproduction and seasonal demand changes are the top drivers of food waste in Uzbekistan
food_waste.uz_fact6_sub: FAO is working with Uzbekistan on a national food waste strategy. The Ministry of Ecology published food waste prevention guidelines.
food_waste.ca_title: Central Asia — a solution is needed
food_waste.ca_card1_body: The UN FAO is implementing "Reduction of Food Loss and Waste in Central Asia, Azerbaijan and Turkey" — covering 7 countries and proving the scale of the problem.
food_waste.ca_card2_body: Uzbekistan is the most populous country in Central Asia with 35+ million people — the biggest opportunity for impact in the region.
food_waste.ca_card3_body: Uzbekistan has committed to SDG 12.3: halving food waste per capita by 2030. Foody Moody directly supports this national goal.
food_waste.env_sec_title: The environmental cost of wasted food
food_waste.env_card1_body: Food decomposing in landfills produces methane — a greenhouse gas 80× more potent than CO₂ over a 20-year period.
food_waste.env_card2_body: Producing food that gets thrown away wastes 250 km³ of water annually — 3× the volume of Lake Geneva.
food_waste.env_card3_body: Wasted food occupies 1.4 billion hectares of agricultural land — almost 30% of the world's total farmland.
food_waste.env_card4_body: If food waste were a country, it would be the 3rd largest emitter of greenhouse gases after the USA and China.
food_waste.help_subtitle: Every stakeholder wins when food is saved — not wasted.
food_waste.help_card1_p2: Reduce your personal food waste footprint
food_waste.help_card2_p1: Earn revenue on food that would be discarded
food_waste.help_card4_p1: First tech solution for food waste in Central Asia
food_waste.cta_subtitle: Join thousands of customers and businesses making a real difference.
food_waste.cta_closing: Every saved meal is a step for our planet.
